item.title: Chart of the Week: Global rates show deeper war fears
item.description: Investors are becoming increasingly hawkish

item.title: We should all be playing pothole politics
item.description: Living in the ‘Potswolds’ has made me one of the Angry Local People pointing at our damaged roads

item.title: Iran war raises the risk of a bond market shock
item.description: Wild swings in UK gilts point to strains that will hurt government finances and jack up borrowing costs for us all

item.title: Uber fights to stay in the robotaxi race
item.description: Ride-hailing company must make up lost ground on driverless vehicles, or risk being left in the rear-view mirror

item.title: History is tragically repeating itself in Lebanon
item.description: Ensuring the country’s survival — and Hizbollah’s weakening — is Trump’s best hope for Middle East success

item.title: Tokaj is so much more than a sweet wine
item.description: Hungary’s most famous wine region has made a huge success of dry whites

item.title: Aston Martin could do with a carpool companion
item.description: Brand still has global cachet but it is finding life tricky as a tiny luxury-car maker

item.title: Games we play as children echo through our adult lives
item.description: What can chess or hide-and-seek teach us about navigating relationships with others?

item.title: Britain must be more vigilant to the risk of sabotage by hostile states
item.description: If Moscow and Beijing are determined to destabilise the west then new strategies are needed

item.title: ‘Vladimir’ and the enduring appeal of campus satire
item.description: Weird rules, shifting cultural politics, contested parking spaces — universities still have all the ingredients for drama

item.title: The new old world
item.description: A Gulf war, an oil spike, a tense Europe: the 20th century is back

item.title: Restaurant Gordon Ramsay High, London: £776 of meh — review
item.description: The sweary chef thinks critics have got it in for him, but it’s his restaurant that is the problem

item.title: A bet on Bill Ackman’s new listing is complex
item.description: The hedge fund manager is seeking to extend his Pershing Square portfolio in multiple vehicles

item.title: Iran war will scar the global economy
item.description: Scenarios that markets and policymakers hoped to avoid are now materialising

item.title: Why Wall Street is scouting college sports stars
item.description: Banks have realised the potential from tapping in to tomorrow’s elite - and by definition wealthy - athletes

item.title: How the oil shock might spread
item.description: Measures undertaken by vulnerable economies may be only the start of things to come

item.title: A prosperous Britain means facing up to trade-offs
item.description: Yes, yes, and yes again to reform of our appalling planning system — but politicians require help to make hard choices

item.title: Mistral CEO: AI companies should pay a content levy in Europe
item.description: A revenue-based charge would protect the livelihoods of copyright holders and bring legal certainty

item.title: Is it time for me to buy equities again?
item.description: Many price/earnings ratios have fallen back to long-run averages

item.title: Can Jane Austen help my teenager?
item.description: The writer’s Regency settings hold lessons for making friends on social media

item.title: Interest rates take fright
item.description: The market’s calm in the face of war is beginning to crack

item.title: The case for slicing up consumer conglomerates further
item.description: Investors are better able to value pure plays, while lacklustre units can dilute overall growth and margins at sprawling companies

item.title: Quiet rituals of survival in Tehran
item.description: War feels like something that should have been left behind in a more brutal century

item.title: The war over tail risks is in full swing
item.description: Trump’s financial fight with insurers will affect business confidence as much as the kinetic battle

item.title: Maritime security is key to global energy supplies
item.description: Companies need to know their vessels can pass through busy shipping lanes repeatedly and safely
